Grady Bunn 02/21/2020 0.1 Addition of Undo Wave , Rebuild Wizard, and logic updates Blake Coram 03/11/2020 0.2 Inclusion of customer specific SKU requirement Blake Coram 03/18/2020 0.3 Updates based on technical review and estimation Blake Coram 04/16/2020 0.4 Updates to include additional examples and assumptions based on review with development team Blake Coram 04/23/2020 0.5 Added two assumptions around PIX generation and use of base PIX configuration/framework EX0 5 – Item Substitution Support for Replenishment McLane sells certain products at different levels of unit of measure (UOM). For instance, the same product could be sold as a full case, repack or single sell. Due to limitations in the McLane host system, when this occurs each unique UOM tracks inventory under a unique item number different from the other UOM’s. So , if a given product sells as case , repack and single sell , then inventory is tracked individually under three item master records. Base WMOS does not support replenishment across item records. However, McLane requires the ability to replenish lower UOM items when required. Specifically, case item inventory can replenish repack item and single sell item inventory, and repack item inventory can replenish single sell item inventory. Additionally, the McLane host and inventory systems track customer specific item inventory which is a duplication of the same UOM. For instance, the case UOM for a particular item could have two item master records, one for a particular customer specific need and one for general use. In this instance also , McLane requires the ability to replenish the customer specific item when necessary. Therefore, as part of this extension WMOS is enhanced to support item substitution and item conversion for replenishment allocation and processing. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) Y Tasks UI, iLPNs UI, and Rebuild Wizard UI as Exception Management MHE N RF Y RF Fill Pick Location and RF Picking/Pulling Shortages Reporting N Other Y Wave Allocation for Replenishment to Support Item Substitution Undo Wave to reverse custom wave replenishments WMOS supports replenishment of the same item across UOM’s and locations. Additionally, WMOS supports Item Substitution for picking/packing allocations. This customization blends these two concepts to allow for item substitution during replenishment allocation. During wave allocation, if WMOS determines that replenishment for demand is required, after first checking base replenishment logic for the item in question , WMOS additionally looks for item substitution opportunities. When identified, WMOS allocates replenishment need for defined substitution item inventory . In this process, the inventory allocated for replenishment to the pick location (To Be Filled) and inventory allocated for picking from the pick location (To Be Picked) are systemically for different items. This is reconciled at the point of filling pick location execution. During the replenishment execution for these item substitution scenarios, the replenishment item and pick location item are not the same. In order to rectify this, WMOS converts the item as part of the replenishment completion such that all inventory tracked in the pick location is the same. At the time of inventory conversion, WMOS generates a new custom PIX to notify the host of the conversion completion. Additionally, exception scenarios exist when performing replenishment execution which must handle the necessary custom updates required in the item substitution scenario. To support the above allocation and conversion substitution requirements, McLane interfaces item substitution details into the item master. WMOS uses these base fields for the necessary custom logic for this extension in determining both which items are valid substitutions for one another as well as the conversion quantities between the items. Assumptions Base item substitution logic is disabled with this extension and not supported in parallel with this extension. No changes to the RF screen flows for replenishment execution are in scope for this extension. No changes to the UI screen flows for waving, replenishment or task maintenance are in scope for this extension. Custom item substitution allocations always break down higher UOM inventory to lower UOM inventory , or like for like UOM inventory . The process is never reversed. Meaning case items can replenish other case items, repack and single sell. Repack items can replenish repack items and single sell. Single sell items can replenish single sell only. But the reverse is not true. Single sell item cannot replenish either case or repack item inventory, and repack item cannot replenish case inventory. All items requiring replenishment through item substitution logic have permanent assigned pick locations. Dynamic pick location assignment and replenishment to dynamic locations are not in scope for this extension. As part of this extension, WMOS converts inventory at the time of fill active. Prior to that point the iLPN inventory remains the substitute item / original item inventory. Custom fill pick location item conversion works with both manual and task execution to convert inventory between substitution items. McLane always fills the full quantity pulled during RF Fill Pick Location. The user is not prompted for quantity to allow for partial filling of the location. All iLPN inventory is single SKU. Any multi-SKU iLPN’s are manually broken down to single SKU prior to custom substitution allocation. The following base Inventory Need Types are in scope for custom item substitution logic: INT 1 – Replenish Active from Reserve INT 9 – Replenish Case Pick from Reserve INT 3 – Replenish Active from Case Pick No new custom replenishment allocations beyond the above defined three INT’s are in scope. For instance, if repack inventory and single sell inventory are both stored in active locations, this extension does not support custom replenishment from active to active. All inventory in WMOS is stored in units. This is applicable to each of the McLane UOM’s of case, repack and single sell. One WMOS unit of case item is equivalent to one case. One WMOS unit of repack item is equivalent to one repack. One WMOS unit of single sell item is equivalent to one single sell. Lean Time Replenishment, RF Replenish , RF LPN Disposition, RF Putaway, and RF Fill Active generation of replenishment allocations are not in scope for this extension. Item Substitution Ratio determines the conversion quantity required between the UOM’s. This value requires a positive non-zero integer, and must be configured from higher UOM to lower UOM. Example 1 : 1 Case = 4 Repacks; 1 Repack = 6 Single Sell Case Item has no item substitutions configured Reasoning is that Case inventory can only be serviced by like Cases and not by a higher UOM Repack Item has one item substitution configured Case item as priority 1 for ratio of 4 – i dentifies available substitute item and quantity conversion requirements Single Sell Item has two item substitutions configured Case item as priority 1 for ratio of 24 – identifies available substitute item, priority sequence for replenishment, and quantity conversion requirements Repack item as priority 2 for ratio 6 – identifies available substitute item, priority sequence for replenishment, and quantity conversion requirements Example 2: 1 Case = 20 Single Sell (no repack) Case item has no item substitution configured Reasoning is that Case inventory can only be serviced by like Cases and not by a higher UOM Single Sell item has one item substitution configured Case item as priority 1 for ratio of 20 – identifies available substitute item and quantity conversion requirements Example 3: 1 Case General Use SKU = 1 Case Customer SKU Case General Use SKU has one item substitution configured Case Custom SKU as priority 1 for ratio of 1 – identifies available substitute item and quantity conversion requirements Case Customer SKU has one item substitution configured Case General Use SKU as priority 1 for ratio of 1 – identifies available substitute item and quantity conversion requirements Example 4: (No Repack) 1 Case General Use SKU = 1 Case Customer SKU; 1 Case General Use SKU = 15 Single Sell General Use; 1 Case Customer SKU = 15 Single Sell Customer SKU ; 1 Case General Use SKU = 15 Single Sell Customer SKU; 1 Case Customer SKU = 15 Single Sell General Use SKU Case General Use SKU has one item substitution configured Case Custom er SKU as priority 1 for ratio of 1 – identifies available substitute item and quantity conversion requirements Case Customer SKU has one item substitution configured Case General Use SKU as priority 1 for ratio of 1 – identifies available substitute item and quantity conversion requirements Single Sell General Use has two item substitutions configured Case General Use SKU as priority 1 for ratio of 15 – identifies available substitute item and quantity conversion requirements Case Customer SKU as priority 2 for ratio of 15 – identifies available substitute item and quantity conversion requirements Single Sell Customer SKU has two item substitutions configured Case Customer SKU as priority 1 for ratio of 15 - identifies available substitute item and quantity conversion requirements Case General Use SKU as priority 2 for ratio of 15 - identifies available substitute item and quantity conversion requirements Once WMOS identifies a source of substitute item replenishment, WMOS allocates what it can from that source substitute item and concludes the custom logic. WMOS does not consider generation of replenishment from multiple substitute items for the same need. Much like base shipping item substitution, WMOS considers immediate substitutions only for this custom replenishment process. For example, if item A (Single Sell) is needed, only items directly associated to substitute for item A are considered (Repacks and Cases). However, chained substitutions are not in scope. Example of chained substitution: Item A (Single Sell) is needed and is replenishable by Item B (Repack) and Item C (Case) Item A has insufficient inventory, and there is a small amount of Item B but not enough to cover the need for Item A Item C has sufficient inventory for directly replenishing Item B which in turn indirectly creates sufficient need for Item A Result is that all inventory of Item B is leveraged for replenishing Item A, but a chain replenishment of C to B to A is not created Soft allocation with the wave is not enhanced as part of this extension. If turned on, soft allocation will only consider the exact item and its inventory. No substitution inventory will be considered in soft allocation. While custom EX05 substitute replenishments are pending for a pick location, the location is temporarily multi-SKU . McLane allows for up to 6 potential custom replenishment item substitutions. The more substitutions that EX05 has to consider the more performance impact to the wave and overall time to complete. No changes to the base process by which overallocation (rounding up to the UOM) is impacted by this extension. The base configuration to round up or not is still supported. No changes to the combination of P40/P50 replenishments are impacted by this extension. Th e P40/P50 combination per base allows combining of replenishments for the same SKU which is still supported. Note, McLane controls this through pick location level configuration settings. The picking wave parameter is always left unchecked for this parameter. WMOS uses MISC_ALPHA _FIELD _1 field on the Allocation and Task detail to t rack custom EX05 substitution replenishments Follow Picking Wave Parameters are assumed static on the McLane wave templates: Active Replenishment Origin – None Note: for McLane this is configured on the item or warehouse level Combine 40/50 Replenishments – Unchecked Note: for McLane this may be checked on the pick location level Bump Up Existing 60/70 Replenishment – Checked Delay Pre-Pick Replenishment – Unchecked Item Substitution - None Perform Reserve Balancing – Unchecked Suppress Post-Pick Replenishment – ‘Select one’ The custom PIX generated at the time of SKU conversion creates in similar form and fashion t o an inventory adjustment PIX for a pick location. Meaning it sends SKU and quantity adjustment information. No LPN or other references are required. The custom PIX generated at the time of SKU conversion leverages the base PIX framework for grouping and export to host. Dependencies Code Dependencies N/A Interface Dependencies Interface Field Requirement Item Substitution ItemSubstitutionHeader  ReqdItemName Defines the originating item Item Substitution ItemSubstitutionHeader  ItemSubstitutionDetail  SubItemName Defines related substitution item Item Substitution ItemSubstitutionHeader  ItemSubstitutionDetail  SubstitutionRank Defines sequence in which custom item substitution logic considers substitute items for replenishment Item Substitution ItemSubstitutionHeader  ItemSubstitutionDetail  SubstitutionRatio Defines ratio between substitute items from higher to lower UOM items P latform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Proces s Flow Diagram Wave Allocation Process Steps WMOS wave allocation identifies opportunities for replenishment generation per base requirement logic . Before any custom replenishment requirements, WMOS first considers base replenishment opportunities for the same item need . All Zone and UOM priorities are considered before moving into custom replenishment. Note it is possible that base replenishment can allocate some but not all of the need. In these situations, the remaining required quantity goes to custom replenishment substitution logic looking to fulfill the remain ing required quantity. If base cannot replenish the same item, WMOS custom logic identifies if the item has available item substitutions. If available, WMOS attempts to allocate substitute item inventory for replenishment item need. All Zone and UOM priorities are considered for the first substitutable item before continuing to the next substitutable item. Only when available inventory is zero for the first substitutable item does WM conti n ue to the next substitutable item, otherwise custom replenishment logic stops for the first substitutable item with available inventory If the custom replenishment for item substitute inventory fails, the process continues with base shortage execution for failed replenishment. Note: Undo Wave reverses tasks and allocations processed in above steps RF Execution and Inventory Conversion Process Steps WMOS replenishment execution from reserve begins with RF Pull iLPN List to pull the iLPN allocated from reserve. This is applicable for both Reserve to Active and Reserve to Case Pick execution. In that process, the user as exception may require to short the pull. When shorting custom item substitution replenishments any custom tracking updates are reverted. Otherwise, if the pull is successful, the user continues the process in the RF Fill Pick Location transaction. WMOS replenishment execution from case pick begins with RF Pack iLPN From Case Pick pulling inventory from the Case Pick Location. This is applicable only for Case Pick to Active replenishment execution. In that process, the user as exception may require to short the pull. When shorting custom item substitution replenishments any custom tracking updates are reverted. Otherwise, if the pull is successful, the user continues the process in the RF Fill Pick Location transaction. For each of the replenishment executions, the RF Fill Pick Location customizations are common. In this process, it is possible the user as exception may require to short the fill. When shorting custom item substitution replenishments any custom tracking updates are reverted. Otherwise, if the fill is successful, custom item substitution allocations require that inventory is converted from the iLPN allocated substitute item to the pick location item . WMOS converts the inventory as part of the fill process and notifies the host of the conversion through a new custom PIX message. Exception Management Process Steps For exception reasons, McLane may require to cancel a replenishment task through the Tasks UI. When this occurs, in addition to the base updates for the task and iLPN if pulled , either the destination location reverts the to be filled quantity as per base for the same item or custom item substitution updates are reverted according to the different item and conversion factor . For exception reasons, McLane may require to cancel a replenishment through de-allocation of the iLPN in the iLPN’s UI. When this occurs, in addition to the base updates for the task/allocation and iLPN , either the destination location reverts the to be filled quantity as per base for the same item or custom item substitution updates are reverted according to the different item and conversion factor . [Need to add to Visio] For exception reasons, McLand may require to Rebuild locations through the Rebuild Wizard to correct allocated or to be filled quantities for the location. Rebuild of allocated quantities are not affected, but rebuild of to be filled q uantities take into account open tasks, allocations, and iLPNs which have a matching destination location, mismatched item, and calculate the rebuilt quantity as per the base quantity multiplied by the custom conversion factor UI Tasks UI – Cancel Replenishment Task For exception reasons, McLane may require to cancel a replenishment task which can be done through the Task s UI. In that scenario, in addition to base WMOS shortage updates, WMOS must also revert any custom item substitution updates performed. Enablement The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX05 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX05 is turned off, and no custom EX05 processing will occur. Validations The following section describes the validations performed by WMOS when the custom oLPN Adjustment is received. First, WMOS validates that the warehouse is appropriately configured to enable the EX05 custom item substitution for replenishment logic. If any of the enablement features are not properly set, WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base task cancelation . Otherwise, WMOS continues to the next validation. Next, WMOS validates the replenishment being shorted is for a custom item substitution need. If the replenishment being shorted is not for custom item substitution, then WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base task cancelation . Otherwise, WMOS continues to the custom item substitution processing below. Updates As part of completing base task cancelation updates, WMOS performs the base updates to cancelation of the task. If the item on the task being canceled is the same as the pick location being replenished, base location updates are also performed. Additionally, the decrementing of the To Be Filled quantity in the pick location is enhanced to decrement the item and quantity based on the item substitution match and ratio, when the items do not match. For instance, the demand item which originated the item substitution replenishment had the to be filled quantity incremented based on the ratio of the item substitution quantity. These custom updates must be reverted when cancelling the replenishment task . Examples Example 1: In this example, the demand item ha d no available inventory for base replenishment and all replenishment generated from single substitute item with more than sufficient inventory. With only one replenishment task generated which is canceled in this process. In that scenario, in addition to base WMOS shortage updates, WMOS must also revert any custom item substitution updates performed. Enablement The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX05 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX05 is turned off, and no custom EX05 processing will occur. Validations The following section describes the validations performed by WMOS when the custom oLPN Adjustment is received. First, WMOS validates that the warehouse is appropriately configured to enable the EX05 custom item substitution for replenishment logic. If any of the enablement features are not properly set, WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base shortage processing. Otherwise, WMOS continues to the next validation. Next, WMOS validates the replenishment being shorted is for a custom item substitution need. If the replenishment being shorted is not for custom item substitution, then WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base short replenishment processing. Otherwise, WMOS continues to the custom item substitution processing below. Updates As part of completing base de-allocation updates, WMOS performs the base updates to cancelation of the allocation or task. If the item being de-allocated is the same as the pick location being replenished, base location updates are also performed. Additionally, the decrementing of the To Be Filled quantity in the pick location is enhanced to decrement the item and quantity based on the item substitution match and ratio, when the items do not match. For instance, the demand item which originated the item substitution replenishment had the to be filled quantity incremented based on the ratio of the item substitution quantity. These custom updates must be reverted when cancelling the replenishment allocation. Rebuild Wizard UI – Rebuild Case Pick or Active Location (To Be Filled) For exception reasons, McLane may require to rebuild misaligned quantities for Case Pick or Active locations through the Rebuild Wizard UI. In that scenario, in addition to base WMOS updates, WMOS must correct any custom item substitution updates performed. Enablement The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX05 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX05 is turned off, and no custom EX05 processing will occur. Validations The following section describes the validations performed by WMOS when the custom oLPN Adjustment is received. First, WMOS validates that the warehouse is appropriately configured to enable the EX05 custom item substitution for rebuild logic. If any of the enablement features are not properly set, WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base rebuild processing. Otherwise, WMOS continues to the next validation. Next, WMOS validates the location being rebuilt is for a custom item substitution need. If the location being rebuilt is not for custom item substitution, then WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base location rebuild processing. Otherwise, WMOS continues to the custom item substitution processing below. Updates As part of completing base rebuild updates for open pick and pull items and quantities , WMOS performs the base updates to allocated quantity . Allocated quantities, regardless of base or custom replenishment , are base for the location rebuild process. A s part of completing base rebuild updates for T o B e F illed items and quantities, WMOS checks the item’s association to open replenishment for base and custom allocations and tasks. If the location being rebuilt has an item the same as the allocation or task open , base location updates are performed. Additionally, the rebuilding of the To Be Filled quantity in the pick location is enhanced to reset the quantity based on the item substitution match and ratio, when the items do not match. For instance, the demand item which originated the item substitution replenishment had the to be filled quantity incremented based on the ratio of the item substitution quantity. Note that for rebuilding Case Pick and Active locations, a mixture of base and custom replenishments is allowed to co-exist, and the rebuild resets the To Be Filled quantity based on the totals across the two. Outputs Activity Tracking No changes to base behavior for Activity Tracking with the location rebuild as part of this extension. PIX No changes to base behavior for PIX generation with the location rebuild as part of this extension. LM Hooks No changes to base behavior for labor management with the location rebuild as part of this extension . MHE No MHE changes in scope for this extension. McLane leverages both tasked and manual replenishment to fill pick locations and both require this custom item conversion. During the fill, WMOS converts the iLPN substituted item to the pick location item as part of the fill completion. Enablement The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX05 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX05 is turned off, and no custom EX05 processing will occur. Last, the transaction specific flag must be enabled. This is controlled through RF Fill Pick Location custom transaction parameter byte 501-501. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX05 is turned off, and no custom EX05 processing will occur. Validations The following section describes the validations performed by WMOS when the custom oLPN Adjustment is received. First, WMOS validates that the warehouse is appropriately configured to enable the EX05 custom item substitution for replenishment logic. If any of the enablement features are not properly set, WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base replenishment processing. Otherwise, WMOS continues to the next validation. Next, WMOS validates the replenishment being completed is for a custom item substitution need. Custom replenishment is based on the iLPN item being different than the pick location item . If the replenishment being completed is not for custom item substitution, then WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base fill pick location processing. Otherwise, WMOS continues to the next validation. Next, WMOS validates the item within the pick location is a valid substitute item for the iLPN item being filled. This is defined in the item substitutions configuration for the iLPN item being filled. If the pick location item does not have the iLPN item listed as a valid substitution item, then WMOS displays custom error message CUST/0501 – “Pick Location Item Not Valid for Item Substitution. ”, and exits custom logic. Otherwise, the iLPN item is a valid substitute item for the pick location item and WMOS continues to the custom item substitution processing below. Updates During the completion of custom substitute item replenishment, the base replenishment process is enhanced to convert the inventory from the iLPN substitute item to the pick location demand item. WMOS consumes the iLPN inventory detail (and iLPN if single- sku iLPN ) per base processing. However, the pick location inventory is not incremented for the iLPN item. Instead WMOS increments the pick location inventory for the substitute demand item. In that process, the quantity in addition to the item must be converted. McLane configures the quantity conversion in the item substitution ratio field. For example, if the iLPN quantity is 5 units and the item conversion ratio for the substitute pick location demand item is 4 , then 20 total units of the pick location demand item would be incremented in the pick location. If the replenishment being completed was for a pending demand allocation or task , WMOS performs the base updates to completion of the allocation or task. In addition, any custom item substitution specific updates must be completed as well. For instance, with allocated or tasked iLPNs both the On Hand should be incremented and the To Be Filled for the pick location demand item should be decremented respectively . Process Change/Addition Wave Replenishment Allocation During wave allocation, McLane generates replenishment allocations for three inventory need types: INT-1 Replenish Active from Reserve, INT-9 Replenish Case Pick from Reserve and INT-3 Replenish Active from Case Pick. At any of these replenishment points, if base WMOS replenishment cannot find sufficient inventory for the demand item, this extension additionally considers new item substitution logic below. When successful, WMOS allocates substitute item inventory for the replenishment need. Enablement The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX05 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX05 is turned off, and no custom EX05 processing will occur. Last, the wave-specific flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the Picking Wave Parameters Processing Attributes, Attribute 1. This field is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX05 is turned off for the wave , and no custom EX05 processing will occur Validations The following section describes the validations performed by WMOS when the custom oLPN Adjustment is received. First, WMOS validates that the warehouse is appropriately configured to enable the EX05 custom item substitution for replenishment logic . If any of the enablement features are not properly set, WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base allocation processing . Otherwise, WMOS continues to the next validation. Next, WMOS validates the demand item requiring replenishment has at least one item substitution of a higher UOM configured. If the demand item requiring replenishment does not have any item substitutions of a higher UOM configured , then WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base allocation processing . Otherwise, WMOS continues to the custom item substitution processing below . Updates The following logic is used to attempt replenishment allocation based on substitute item inventory . Note, a given demand item can have multiple item substitutions configured, and WMOS considers each substitute item in item substitution rank/priority sequence. WMOS identifies these item substitutions based on the demand item being configured as an available item substitution for another item in WMOS. For example, McLane supports up to three UOM levels: case, repack and single sell. Valid item substitutions are as follows: case to case; case to repack; repack to repack; case to single sell; repack to single sell ; single sell to single sell . These are identified by : the single sell item s having configured item substitutions of case item, repack item , single sell item , or all o f these with respective ratio for conversion ; the repack items having configured item substitutions for case item, repack item, or both with respective ratio for conversion ; the case items having configured item substitutions for case item or nothing . Item Item UOM Type (Item Reference Field) Substitutable Item (Replenishable UOM) Rank Ratio ITEM_ 1_ A CASE ITEM_ 1_ B REPACK ITEM_ 1_ A 1 4 ITEM_ 1_ C SINGLE SELL ITEM_ 1_ A 1 24 ITEM_ 1_ B 2 6 ITEM_ 2_ D CASE ITEM_ 2_ E REPACK ITEM_ 2_ D 1 10 ITEM_ 2_ F SINGLE SELL ITEM_ 2_ D 1 50 ITEM_ 3_ G CASE ITEM_ 3_ H REPACK ITEM_ 3_ G 1 5 ITEM_ 3_ I SINGLE SELL ITEM_ 3_ H 1 40 ITEM_ 4_ J SINGLE SELL In the example: ITEM_1_C can be replenished by either ITEM_1 variations of higher package , with a defined priority sequence ITEM_2_F can only be replenished by Case inventory of ITEM_2, not allowed to be replenished by Repack ITEM_ 3 _ I can only be replenished by Repack inventory of ITEM_ 3 , not allowed to be replenished by Case ITEM_ 4 _ J has no substitutable definitions because it is only ever maintained and sold by Single Sells For each substitute item configured for the demand item, WMOS attempts to identify replenishment opportunities. A new allocation need for the substitute item is created for the converted demand item replenishment need quantity. The converted need quantity depends on the configured item substitution ratio. For example, it is possible WMOS replenishes case item inventory as the replenishment item for a repack item as the demand item. In this case, the case and repack inventory are not one to one, but defined as a ratio on the item substitution. If this ratio is defined as 4 , then for each unit of case inventory allocated for replenishment, four units of repack inventory can be picked. So, in this example, a total demand item need for 2 0 units of repack item inventory, would result in replenishment need for 5 units of case item inventory. When successful, WMOS generates replenishment allocation for the substitute item to the demand item pick location. The substitute item replenishment must also account for the demand item to be filled quantity such that the necessary to be picked quantities can be allocated. This includes conversion of the quantity based on the ratio. As in the above example, the repack item demand was for 2 0 units which resulted in replenishment of only 5 units of case item inventory based on the item substitution ratio of 4 . Therefore, the to be filled for the repack item should still be 2 0 units to allow for picking demand up to the full repack item quantity. Per base, WMOS generates replenishments only for the same item. In th ese instances, the demand item requiring picking and the replenishment item being filled are the same. The To Be Picked quantity allocated from the pick location goes against the pending To Be Filled quantity for the replenishment. In a base replenishment scenario, this would be all against the same Pick Location Detail and WM Inventory records for the same item. In EX05 custom substitute replenishment, the demand and replenishment items are not the same. The demand item requires incremental To Be Filled quantity in order for base to allow To Be Picked allocations from the location. That To Be Filled quantity for the demand item will not have any associated alloc ation or replenishment . This To Be Filled quantity is custom updated at the time the substitute item replenishment is created. The substitute item replenishment itself also has associated To Be Filled quantity for the substitute item associated to the substitute replenishment. When generating the substitute replenishment, it is possible WMOS must create a net new record for Pick Location Detail and WM Inventory for that substitute item as it did not previously exist. The new pick location detail is created with maximum capacity of zero to avoid any other putaway possibilities and WM inventory record is created with TBF=TBP to nullify the dummy to be filled created. Sequencing of allocation follows the configured allocation parameters and priorities as relevant to the wave template/order/item hierarchy in base. All zones and UOMs are attempted in configured sequence for the base item before moving onto the first custom substitute item, and all zones and UOMs are attempted in the same sequence before moving onto the second custom substitute item, continuing for all configured substitution priorities. If allocation for the substitute item fails and other substitute items exist for the demand item, then WMOS continues to the next substitute item in sequence. If no more substitute items exist for the demand item, then WMOS concludes the custom logic and continues with base processing. Additionally, in this process WMOS updates the MISC_ALPHA _FIELD _1 field for tracking the EX05 replenishments with a value of EX05 the Item Id and Quantity required for the demand item . WMOS concatenates these values with a pipe delimiter in the Misc. field. Undo Wave – Revert Wave Replenishment Allocation During wave allocation, McLane generates replenishment allocations for three inventory need types: INT-1 Replenish Active from Reserve, INT-9 Replenish Case Pick from Reserve and INT-3 Replenish Active from Case Pick. After successful allocation either through base or custom allocation, the wave may be undone by the operations. Undoing a wave revert all base and custom replenishment allocations and tasks. Enablement The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX05 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX05 is turned off, and no custom EX05 processing will occur. Last, the wave-specific flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the Picking Wave Parameters Processing Attributes, Attribute 1. This field is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX05 is turned off for the wave, and no custom EX05 processing will occur Validations The following section describes the validations performed by WMOS when the custom oLPN Adjustment is received. First, WMOS validates that the warehouse is appropriately configured to enable the EX05 custom item substitution for replenishment logic. If any of the enablement features are not properly set, WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base de- allocation processing. Otherwise, WMOS continues to the next validation. Next, WMOS validates the replenishment being undone is for a custom item substitution need. If the replenishment being undone is not for custom item substitution, then WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base de-allocation . Otherwise, WMOS continues to the custom item substitution processing below. Updates As part of completing base undo wave updates, WMOS performs the base updates to cancelation of the task and allocation . If the item on the task or allocation being undone is the same as the pick location being replenished, base location updates are also performed. Additionally, the decrementing of the To Be Filled quantity in the pick location is enhanced to decrement the item and quantity based on the item substitution match and ratio, when the items do not match. For instance, the demand item which originated the item substitution replenishment had the to be filled quantity incremented based on the ratio of the item substitution quantity. These custom updates must be reverted when undoing a wave containing custom replenishment task s or allocations . Any WM I nventory record or Pick Location Detail record created by the custom replen ishment wave allocation will be deleted after Undo Wave is performed. Grady Bunn 02/21/2020 0.1 Addition of Undo Wave , Rebuild Wizard, and logic updates Blake Coram 03/11/2020 0.2 Inclusion of customer specific SKU requirement Blake Coram 03/18/2020 0.3 Updates based on technical review and estimation Blake Coram 04/16/2020 0.4 Updates to include additional examples and assumptions based on review with development team Blake Coram 04/23/2020 0.5 Added two assumptions around PIX generation and use of base PIX configuration/framework EX0 5 – Item Substitution Support for Replenishment McLane sells certain products at different levels of unit of measure (UOM). For instance, the same product could be sold as a full case, repack or single sell. Due to limitations in the McLane host system, when this occurs each unique UOM tracks inventory under a unique item number different from the other UOM’s. So , if a given product sells as case , repack and single sell , then inventory is tracked individually under three item master records. Base WMOS does not support replenishment across item records. However, McLane requires the ability to replenish lower UOM items when required. Specifically, case item inventory can replenish repack item and single sell item inventory, and repack item inventory can replenish single sell item inventory. Additionally, the McLane host and inventory systems track customer specific item inventory which is a duplication of the same UOM. For instance, the case UOM for a particular item could have two item master records, one for a particular customer specific need and one for general use. In this instance also , McLane requires the ability to replenish the customer specific item when necessary. Therefore, as part of this extension WMOS is enhanced to support item substitution and item conversion for replenishment allocation and processing.

Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) Y Tasks UI, iLPNs UI, and Rebuild Wizard UI as Exception Management MHE N RF Y RF Fill Pick Location and RF Picking/Pulling Shortages Reporting N Other Y Wave Allocation for Replenishment to Support Item Substitution Undo Wave to reverse custom wave replenishments WMOS supports replenishment of the same item across UOM’s and locations. Additionally, WMOS supports Item Substitution for picking/packing allocations. This customization blends these two concepts to allow for item substitution during replenishment allocation. During wave allocation, if WMOS determines that replenishment for demand is required, after first checking base replenishment logic for the item in question , WMOS additionally looks for item substitution opportunities. When identified, WMOS allocates replenishment need for defined substitution item inventory . In this process, the inventory allocated for replenishment to the pick location (To Be Filled) and inventory allocated for picking from the pick location (To Be Picked) are systemically for different items. This is reconciled at the point of filling pick location execution. During the replenishment execution for these item substitution scenarios, the replenishment item and pick location item are not the same. In order to rectify this, WMOS converts the item as part of the replenishment completion such that all inventory tracked in the pick location is the same. At the time of inventory conversion, WMOS generates a new custom PIX to notify the host of the conversion completion. Additionally, exception scenarios exist when performing replenishment execution which must handle the necessary custom updates required in the item substitution scenario. To support the above allocation and conversion substitution requirements, McLane interfaces item substitution details into the item master. WMOS uses these base fields for the necessary custom logic for this extension in determining both which items are valid substitutions for one another as well as the conversion quantities between the items. Assumptions Base item substitution logic is disabled with this extension and not supported in parallel with this extension. No changes to the RF screen flows for replenishment execution are in scope for this extension.

tutions for one another as well as the conversion quantities between the items. Assumptions Base item substitution logic is disabled with this extension and not supported in parallel with this extension. No changes to the RF screen flows for replenishment execution are in scope for this extension.

No changes to the UI screen flows for waving, replenishment or task maintenance are in scope for this extension. Custom item substitution allocations always break down higher UOM inventory to lower UOM inventory , or like for like UOM inventory . The process is never reversed. Meaning case items can replenish other case items, repack and single sell. Repack items can replenish repack items and single sell. Single sell items can replenish single sell only. But the reverse is not true. Single sell item cannot replenish either case or repack item inventory, and repack item cannot replenish case inventory. All items requiring replenishment through item substitution logic have permanent assigned pick locations. Dynamic pick location assignment and replenishment to dynamic locations are not in scope for this extension. As part of this extension, WMOS converts inventory at the time of fill active. Prior to that point the iLPN inventory remains the substitute item / original item inventory. Custom fill pick location item conversion works with both manual and task execution to convert inventory between substitution items. McLane always fills the full quantity pulled during RF Fill Pick Location. The user is not prompted for quantity to allow for partial filling of the location. All iLPN inventory is single SKU. Any multi-SKU iLPN’s are manually broken down to single SKU prior to custom substitution allocation. The following base Inventory Need Types are in scope for custom item substitution logic: INT 1 – Replenish Active from Reserve INT 9 – Replenish Case Pick from Reserve INT 3 – Replenish Active from Case Pick No new custom replenishment allocations beyond the above defined three INT’s are in scope. For instance, if repack inventory and single sell inventory are both stored in active locations, this extension does not support custom replenishment from active to active. All inventory in WMOS is stored in units. This is applicable to each of the McLane UOM’s of case, repack and single sell. One WMOS unit of case item is equivalent to one case. One WMOS unit of repack item is equivalent to one repack. One WMOS unit of single sell item is equivalent to one single sell. Lean Time Replenishment, RF Replenish , RF LPN Disposition, RF Putaway, and RF Fill Active generation of replenishment allocations are not in scope for this extension. Item Substitution Ratio determines the conversion quantity required between the UOM’s.

substitute item and quantity conversion requirements Single Sell Customer SKU has two item substitutions configured Case Customer SKU as priority 1 for ratio of 15 - identifies available substitute item and quantity conversion requirements Case General Use SKU as priority 2 for ratio of 15 - identifies available substitute item and quantity conversion requirements Once WMOS identifies a source of substitute item replenishment, WMOS allocates what it can from that source substitute item and concludes the custom logic. WMOS does not consider generation of replenishment from multiple substitute items for the same need. Much like base shipping item substitution, WMOS considers immediate substitutions only for this custom replenishment process. For example, if item A (Single Sell) is needed, only items directly associated to substitute for item A are considered (Repacks and Cases). However, chained substitutions are not in scope. Example of chained substitution: Item A (Single Sell) is needed and is replenishable by Item B (Repack) and Item C (Case) Item A has insufficient inventory, and there is a small amount of Item B but not enough to cover the need for Item A Item C has sufficient inventory for directly replenishing Item B which in turn indirectly creates sufficient need for Item A Result is that all inventory of Item B is leveraged for replenishing Item A, but a chain replenishment of C to B to A is not created Soft allocation with the wave is not enhanced as part of this extension. If turned on, soft allocation will only consider the exact item and its inventory. No substitution inventory will be considered in soft allocation. While custom EX05 substitute replenishments are pending for a pick location, the location is temporarily multi-SKU . McLane allows for up to 6 potential custom replenishment item substitutions. The more substitutions that EX05 has to consider the more performance impact to the wave and overall time to complete. No changes to the base process by which overallocation (rounding up to the UOM) is impacted by this extension. The base configuration to round up or not is still supported. This is applicable for both Reserve to Active and Reserve to Case Pick execution. In that process, the user as exception may require to short the pull. When shorting custom item substitution replenishments any custom tracking updates are reverted. Otherwise, if the pull is successful, the user continues the process in the RF Fill Pick Location transaction. WMOS replenishment execution from case pick begins with RF Pack iLPN From Case Pick pulling inventory from the Case Pick Location. When shorting custom item substitution replenishments any custom tracking updates are reverted. Otherwise, if the pull is successful, the user continues the process in the RF Fill Pick Location transaction. For each of the replenishment executions, the RF Fill Pick Location customizations are common. In this process, it is possible the user as exception may require to short the fill. When shorting custom item substitution replenishments any custom tracking updates are reverted. Otherwise, if the fill is successful, custom item substitution allocations require that inventory is converted from the iLPN allocated substitute item to the pick location item . WMOS converts the inventory as part of the fill process and notifies the host of the conversion through a new custom PIX message. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX05 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX05 is turned off, and no custom EX05 processing will occur. First, WMOS validates that the warehouse is appropriately configured to enable the EX05 custom item substitution for replenishment logic. If any of the enablement features are not properly set, WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base replenishment processing. Otherwise, WMOS continues to the next validation. Next, WMOS validates the replenishment being completed is for a custom item substitution need. Custom replenishment is based on the iLPN item being different than the pick location item . If the replenishment being completed is not for custom item substitution, then WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base fill pick location processing. Otherwise, WMOS continues to the next validation. Next, WMOS validates the item within the pick location is a valid substitute item for the iLPN item being filled.

ing completed is not for custom item substitution, then WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base fill pick location processing. Otherwise, WMOS continues to the next validation. Next, WMOS validates the item within the pick location is a valid substitute item for the iLPN item being filled.

This is defined in the item substitutions configuration for the iLPN item being filled. If the pick location item does not have the iLPN item listed as a valid substitution item, then WMOS displays custom error message CUST/0501 – “Pick Location Item Not Valid for Item Substitution. ”, and exits custom logic. Otherwise, the iLPN item is a valid substitute item for the pick location item and WMOS continues to the custom item substitution processing below. Updates During the completion of custom substitute item replenishment, the base replenishment process is enhanced to convert the inventory from the iLPN substitute item to the pick location demand item. WMOS consumes the iLPN inventory detail (and iLPN if single- sku iLPN ) per base processing. However, the pick location inventory is not incremented for the iLPN item. Instead WMOS increments the pick location inventory for the substitute demand item. In that process, the quantity in addition to the item must be converted. McLane configures the quantity conversion in the item substitution ratio field. For example, if the iLPN quantity is 5 units and the item conversion ratio for the substitute pick location demand item is 4 , then 20 total units of the pick location demand item would be incremented in the pick location. If the replenishment being completed was for a pending demand allocation or task , WMOS performs the base updates to completion of the allocation or task. In addition, any custom item substitution specific updates must be completed as well. For instance, with allocated or tasked iLPNs both the On Hand should be incremented and the To Be Filled for the pick location demand item should be decremented respectively . RF Fill Pick Location – Short During replenishment execution, the filling of the pick location, both active and case pick, leverages RF Fill Pick Location to execute the completion of the fill. In that process, as exception, the user may short the replenishment fill. In that scenario, in addition to base WMOS shortage updates, WMOS must also revert any custom item substitution updates performed. Enablement The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX05 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX05 is turned off, and no custom EX05 processing will occur. Last, the transaction specific flag must be enabled. This is controlled through RF Fill Pick Location custom transaction parameter byte 501-501. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX05 is turned off, and no custom EX05 processing will occur. First, WMOS validates that the warehouse is appropriately configured to enable the EX05 custom item substitution for replenishment logic . If any of the enablement features are not properly set, WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base allocation processing . Otherwise, WMOS continues to the next validation. Next, WMOS validates the demand item requiring replenishment has at least one item substitution of a higher UOM configured. If the demand item requiring replenishment does not have any item substitutions of a higher UOM configured , then WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base allocation processing . Otherwise, WMOS continues to the custom item substitution processing below . Updates The following logic is used to attempt replenishment allocation based on substitute item inventory . Note, a given demand item can have multiple item substitutions configured, and WMOS considers each substitute item in item substitution rank/priority sequence. WMOS identifies these item substitutions based on the demand item being configured as an available item substitution for another item in WMOS. For example, McLane supports up to three UOM levels: case, repack and single sell. Valid item substitutions are as follows: case to case; case to repack; repack to repack; case to single sell; repack to single sell ; single sell to single sell . These are identified by : the single sell item s having configured item substitutions of case item, repack item , single sell item , or all o f these with respective ratio for conversion ; the repack items having configured item substitutions for case item, repack item, or both with respective ratio for conversion ; the case items having configured item substitutions for case item or nothing .

A new allocation need for the substitute item is created for the converted demand item replenishment need quantity. The converted need quantity depends on the configured item substitution ratio. For example, it is possible WMOS replenishes case item inventory as the replenishment item for a repack item as the demand item. In this case, the case and repack inventory are not one to one, but defined as a ratio on the item substitution. If this ratio is defined as 4 , then for each unit of case inventory allocated for replenishment, four units of repack inventory can be picked. So, in this example, a total demand item need for 2 0 units of repack item inventory, would result in replenishment need for 5 units of case item inventory. When successful, WMOS generates replenishment allocation for the substitute item to the demand item pick location. The substitute item replenishment must also account for the demand item to be filled quantity such that the necessary to be picked quantities can be allocated. This includes conversion of the quantity based on the ratio. As in the above example, the repack item demand was for 2 0 units which resulted in replenishment of only 5 units of case item inventory based on the item substitution ratio of 4 . Therefore, the to be filled for the repack item should still be 2 0 units to allow for picking demand up to the full repack item quantity. Per base, WMOS generates replenishments only for the same item.

In th ese instances, the demand item requiring picking and the replenishment item being filled are the same. The To Be Picked quantity allocated from the pick location goes against the pending To Be Filled quantity for the replenishment. In a base replenishment scenario, this would be all against the same Pick Location Detail and WM Inventory records for the same item. In EX05 custom substitute replenishment, the demand and replenishment items are not the same. The demand item requires incremental To Be Filled quantity in order for base to allow To Be Picked allocations from the location. That To Be Filled quantity for the demand item will not have any associated alloc ation or replenishment . This To Be Filled quantity is custom updated at the time the substitute item replenishment is created. The substitute item replenishment itself also has associated To Be Filled quantity for the substitute item associated to the substitute replenishment. When generating the substitute replenishment, it is possible WMOS must create a net new record for Pick Location Detail and WM Inventory for that substitute item as it did not previously exist. The new pick location detail is created with maximum capacity of zero to avoid any other putaway possibilities and WM inventory record is created with TBF=TBP to nullify the dummy to be filled created. Sequencing of allocation follows the configured allocation parameters and priorities as relevant to the wave template/order/item hierarchy in base. All zones and UOMs are attempted in configured sequence for the base item before moving onto the first custom substitute item, and all zones and UOMs are attempted in the same sequence before moving onto the second custom substitute item, continuing for all configured substitution priorities. If allocation for the substitute item fails and other substitute items exist for the demand item, then WMOS continues to the next substitute item in sequence. If no more substitute items exist for the demand item, then WMOS concludes the custom logic and continues with base processing. Additionally, in this process WMOS updates the MISC_ALPHA _FIELD _1 field for tracking the EX05 replenishments with a value of EX05 the Item Id and Quantity required for the demand item . WMOS concatenates these values with a pipe delimiter in the Misc. field.
